nodes.title: regarding torch.compile
widgets_values: torch.compile added, when it works and you do one or two gens , the remaining ones are visibly faster, just make sure to do the first gen or two at 1-2 steps so it doesn't take much time. (it is disabled by default)

nodes.title: model
widgets_values:
I recommend DISTILLED model particularly gguf versions of ltxvideo 2.3

(https://huggingface.co/unsloth/LTX-2.3-GGUF/tree/main/distilled)

You can run it in around 8-10 steps with the single stage workflow. (this is a modified workflow from Kijai. it doesn't do second stage so no upscaling.)

For clip(s) : Use the fp8 one

(https://huggingface.co/Comfy-Org/ltx-2/blob/main/split_files/text_encoders/gemma_3_12B_it_fp8_scaled.safetensors) 

-tried gguf's , it doesn't make much difference with this clip, it is very demanding , especially if you are using clip realtime on the workflow aka not caching prompts beforehand. So stick with fp8 at least it is as best as you can get)

and this for the second clip model ;

(https://huggingface.co/Kijai/LTX2.3_comfy/blob/main/text_encoders/ltx-2.3_text_projection_bf16.safetensors)

video vae & audio vae : 

(https://huggingface.co/Kijai/LTX2.3_comfy/tree/main/vae)

spatio temporal tiled vae decode :
it is from ltxvideo themselves, available with their nodes. key point is how many spatial tiles you use, if you enter 2 it would 2x2 etc. VRAM usage would be lower but it would that a bit longer but not as bad as using normal vae encode and getting OOM in the end of a long run ...

cfz-start :
helps with clearing terminal if you want, also if add it to other nodes, it will calculate how much time has passed between any two node or the full workflow. Finally can enable/disable cudnn.IF USING THIS WF WITH ZLUDA MAKE SURE TO DISABLE CUDNN ON THIS NODE.

resolution :
the number entered is applied to both height and width of input image and resized (multiples of 16, better for wan), or can use any resolution manually of course (always make sure multiples of 16)

length in seconds : 
ltx 2.3 can do 10 seconds or more.

positive & negative cached :
these are made with my cfz-caching nodes. You can of course use the usual clip loader-clip text encode way for both positive and negative, but if you do lots of gens with same prompt or at least use the same negative , you can create it and save it with the group named -prompt creation- and then refresh the window so you can see it on the "prompt" or "negative". To just run and generate and save prompts in that group, fill the text encode, give it a name in save conditioning and "execute" the preview as text node. This will let you generate prompts again and again and later use them on the workflow. AND all of this means you don't need to spend additional vram, system ram or time to use the clip model. (This is much important in ltx because clip is heavier on vram and gpu)

nodes.title: nodes needed
widgets_values:
* cfz-caching (from comfyui-zluda)
* kjnodes
* WanVideoWrapper
* comfyui-videohelpersuite
* ltxvideo (for the great spatio temporal tiled vae node)
*NEW*
* added "rife frame interpolation" , this is a different node from most people use and it uses a different model. Just made sure cudnn is disabled when running it through, otherwise first run takes a while.
- https://github.com/GACLove/ComfyUI-VFI -

nodes.title: !!! IMPORTANT NOTE !!!
widgets_values:
* MAKE SURE YOU AREN'T USING "ovum-cudnn-wrapper" custom node. It interferes with ltxaudio node. Since we already have cudnn disabling nodes on CFZ with a bit of extra work , automatic disabling isn't worth it.

*** IMPORTANT ***
-Try these commandline parameters when running comfyui ; I am using them solely for ltxvideo (for others such as wan they help IF I go for higher resolution)

* "--use-quad-cross-attention --disable-api-nodes --cache-none --disable-smart-memory --disable-pinned-memory --disable-dynamic-vram --reserve-vram 16" 

yes it reserves all 16 gb (change it to your gpu's vram amount) and yes it helps* 

Experiment with these parameters, for example quad-cross-attention might not be needed for the newer 7000s and 9000s.

nodes.title: SETTINGS
widgets_values:
* fps : 24, 25, or 30.
* length in seconds : ltxv 2.3 cando over 10 seconds.
* resolution : it is applied on both height and width of the imput image. best suitable for ltxv is selected.

*** ADVANCED ***

* try "shift 13.00, sampler lcm, scheduler linear _quadratic and steps 10 for a different approach.

-

* spatial-tiles are set to 3 by default, it determines how the decoding is tiled, for resolutios below 640x it is OK but if you want to push higher try setting it to higher numbers OR you will get OOM in vae decoding stage ... If you are going to try higher resolutions it is best to try with 1 step to see if it can decode.

(this is with my recommended comfyui startup parameters)

* "--use-quad-cross-attention --disable-api-nodes --cache-none --disable-smart-memory --disable-pinned-memory --disable-dynamic-vram --reserve-vram 16" 

yes it reserves all 16 gb (change it to your gpu's vram amount) and yes it helps* 

Experiment with these parameters, for example quad-cross-attention might not be needed for the newer 7000s and 9000s.
